scope.summary: CC2 replaces the shared HTTP-result Condvar with per-session notification state, so an event wakes only the session that received it while removal and shutdown still wake their affected waiters.
out_of_scope: SSE framing, result retention, and authentication behavior are unchanged.

entry_points.name: HttpResultStore::append_event_if_session / wait_for_result
entry_points.trace: Implementing commit be9c64bc stores an Arc<Condvar> with each HttpResultSession. The append path notifies that session only; remove_session notifies its removed session and close_all collects and notifies every session. result_store_wakes_only_affected_session_waiters records per-session waiter rechecks: its target receives the event, three unrelated waiters remain at zero rechecks, remove_session returns Closed, and close_all releases the remainder.

entry_points.name: scoped compile verification
entry_points.trace: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/target-lavendertern cargo check -p oraclemcp-core --tests --locked completed successfully after the implementation, compiling the CC2 regression test with the crate test target.

limitations: Scoped cargo check compiled the test target; the runtime test suite was not run because the operator limited this wave to scoped cargo check commands.
No live HTTP server was exercised; this is deterministic in-process result-store coverage.
